steps.prompt: Rooks slide along ranks and files. Move the rook up to a4.
steps.success: Straight up the file — that's the rook's power.

steps.prompt: Rooks slide sideways too. Take the rook across to h4.
steps.success: The rook controls whole ranks and files at once.

steps.prompt: Rooks capture the same way they move. Take the pawn on a7.
steps.success: Captured! A rook is worth about five pawns.

steps.prompt: Bishops slide along diagonals. Move the bishop to g5.
steps.success: Nice — c1, d2, e3, f4, g5: one clean diagonal.
A bishop stays on its color for the whole game.

steps.prompt: Knights move in an L: two squares one way, one square across. Jump to c3.
steps.success: That L-shape is the knight's signature.

steps.prompt: Knights are the only piece that can jump over others. Hop over your pawns to d2.
steps.success: Nothing blocks a knight — it jumps clean over.

steps.prompt: The queen moves like a rook AND a bishop. Slide her up to d7.
Now the bishop side: take the diagonal to h5.
steps.success: Your most powerful piece — worth about nine pawns.

steps.prompt: Pawns move straight ahead — and may go two squares on their first move. Push the pawn to e4.
steps.success: After the first move, pawns advance one square at a time.

steps.prompt: Pawns capture diagonally, not straight. Take the pawn on d4.
steps.success: Diagonal captures — the pawn's only sideways step.

steps.prompt: Check means the king is under attack. Move your rook to attack the black king.
steps.success: Check! The king must deal with the threat immediately.

steps.prompt: Your king is in check from the rook! Step out of its line of attack.
steps.success: Safe! You can escape check by moving, blocking, or capturing the attacker.

steps.prompt: Checkmate ends the game: check with no escape. The black king is trapped behind its own pawns — deliver mate with the rook.
steps.success: Checkmate! The king can't escape its own pawn wall. This is the classic back-rank mate.

steps.prompt: Good openings fight for the center. Push a center pawn two squares.
steps.success: The center controls the game — your pieces reach more squares from there.

steps.prompt: Now develop a piece toward the center — bring out a knight to f3.
steps.success: Develop knights and bishops early, castle soon, and don't move the same piece twice without reason.
